verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 is an intracellular inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor and calcium channel located on the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) membrane. It functions as an ER-resident TM protein mediating Ca2+ release from intracellular stores in response to IP3 signaling. The protein is embedded in the ER membrane bilayer with its ligand-binding domain in the cytoplasmic/ER-luminal space and calcium-channel domain at the C-terminus — topology intrinsic to ER function. No documented cell-state-induced surface display, tissue-restricted surface presentation, lysosomal exocytosis, dual PM/ER localization, or stable surface-attachment mechanism has been reported for ITPR3. The protein has no TM anchor of its own to the plasma membrane and is not a stable partner of any known surface receptor. It remains endomembrane-resident under all documented biological conditions.
